signals.headline: Powell (late 2025/early 2026 remarks) called the economy "very unusual" with tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 20
signals.raw: Powell (late 2025/early 2026 remarks) called the economy "very unusual" with tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026, labor market possibly weaker than data shows (payrolls overstating by ~60k/month), and non-tariff inflation near 2%; he noted tariffs already fueling early-2026 goods inflation.[2][5][6]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: IMF's April 2 Article IV Consultation projected U
signals.raw: IMF's April 2 Article IV Consultation projected U.S. GDP at 2.4% for 2026, core PCE inflation at 2.6% by year-end (vs. 2% target in early 2027), fed funds rate easing minimally from 3.6% to 3.4%, and tariffs reducing activity amid high debt (123.9% of GDP).[1]

signals.headline: Mavericks defeat Lakers 134-128 (April 21)** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Mavericks defeat Lakers 134-128 (April 21)** | YES | HIGH confidence
Cooper Flagg scored 45 points with 9 assists and 8 rebounds, following his 51-point performance two nights earlier.[1][2] The Mavericks ended a 14-game home losing streak.[2]

signals.headline: Lakers clinched playoff berth** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Lakers clinched playoff berth** | YES | HIGH confidence
Despite injuries to Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, the Lakers remain playoff-bound and dropped into a tie for third in the Western Conference.[2]

signals.headline: Flagg leads Rookie of the Year race** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Flagg leads Rookie of the Year race** | YES | HIGH confidence
Flagg is described as the "clear-cut Rookie of the Year" after consecutive 40+ point games.[3] He scored 96 points across back-to-back games, the most by a rookie since Wilt Chamberlain.[4]
